model.quantization: Float16 encoder weights; Float32 mel filter
model.license: MIT source model and official converter; pinned converted model card has no structured license field

runtime.boundary: Rust f32 PCM and owned JSON through a repository-owned task-level C ABI to the official MLX C++ core

energy.notes: No alternating powermetrics run was captured for the encoder-only spike.

engineering_cost.conversion_effort: The official MLX Examples conversion produced the pinned NPZ. The repository fetcher verifies it and extracts the 66 encoder NPY tensors plus the official mel filter; product inference contains no Python or Node logic.
engineering_cost.update_cost: The same adapter source compiled and produced identical fingerprints on official MLX v0.31.2 and v0.32.0. The upgrade required zero source edits.
engineering_cost.packaging_cost: For v0.32.0 the statically linked shim is 18,638,120 bytes and the required macOS-14 metallib is 130,164,152 bytes. Runtime also needs an executable-relative RPATH and CPU materialization before GPU weight copies.

result.blocker: The complete real audio encoder is proven, but tokenizer, autoregressive decoder, timestamps, cancellation between decoder steps, and transcript quality are not implemented; encoder timing must not be presented as end-to-end ASR.
observations: The official MLX core is technically viable as the third first-class inference foundation from Rust.
Three load/run/destroy cycles completed on both CPU and GPU, with two runs each and stable fingerprints.
The median second-run GPU encoder time was 11.754 ms for 10.56 seconds of real audio; CPU was 115.136 ms.
The Metal Float16 fingerprint exactly matched the official MLX Examples oracle; the CPU path uses its supported Float32 behavior after source-built CPU Float16 diverged in the first convolution.
The v0.31.2 to v0.32.0 upgrade required no adapter changes and preserved output fingerprints.
mlx-c did not answer an unresolved question in this spike, so it was not added as a second dependency path.
